description: Durable confirmatory-readiness and analysis-freeze governance record for the Lumen Route A study. Created at Phase 6B-2 closure, prior to full data collection.

test_subject_A.max_output_tokens: to be recorded on first full run
test_subject_A.access_date: to be recorded on first full run

test_subject_B.max_output_tokens: to be recorded on first full run
test_subject_B.access_date: to be recorded on first full run

model_freeze.separation_constraint: claude-sonnet-4-6 (contract-generation LLM) must not be used as a test subject. Verified: neither frozen test subject is claude-sonnet-4-6.

confirmatory_family.primary_endpoint: H1 on T2 (bug detection composite score 0-3)
confirmatory_family.aggregation: per-function mean score across both frozen models before testing

full_t2_plan.description: Pre-validated full T2 confirmatory run plan for 30 retained functions × 5 conditions × 2 models.
full_t2_plan.planner_command: PYTHONPATH=src /Users/takumi/.pyenv/versions/3.11.5/bin/python3 -m experiment.run_pilot --models gpt-5.4 claude-opus-4-6 --tasks T2 --conditions C1 C1+ C2 C3 C4 --run-id full_t2_confirmatory --dry-run

manifest_flags_post_6b1.note: C1+ and C3/C4 availability flags updated in functions_manifest.json by Phase 6B-1

t2_test_quality.note: All 30 retained T2 test files upgraded to live tests in Phase 6B-3 (2026-04-08). Each file has at least two real test methods with assertions. Previously 18 stubs; all closed.

reproducibility_record.note: Access dates and max output token limits for gpt-5.4 and claude-opus-4-6 were filled in docs/experimental-protocol.md §8.2 in commit 16269a2 (2026-04-09). Same MAX_TOKENS=1024 in src/utils/llm_client.py governed both the archived attempt 1 and the authoritative attempt 2 (full_t2_confirmatory_v2).

t1_t3_full_runs.note: This governance record covers T2 only. T1 and T3 full runs require separate planning once T2 confirmatory analysis is complete.

confirmatory_run_attempts.description: Authoritative log of full confirmatory T2 launch attempts. Only one attempt may be marked AUTHORITATIVE at a time. Failed attempts are preserved as diagnostic-only and must not be used as input to confirmatory closure.

failure_classes.evidence: index.json failure_reason 'unhandled_runner_error: [Errno 60] Operation timed out' raised in src/experiment/runner.py:388 (resolve_representation_artifact -> pathlib.read_text)

index.json failure_reason 'model_invocation_failed: TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for |: type and NoneType' from the original 2026-04-08/09 Python-3.9 first attempt; the 2026-04-25/26 resume crashed in _write_analysis_export at runner.py:1137 with [Errno 60] before iterating to these tail-of-queue items

protocol_blocker.evidence: src/experiment/score_t2.py modified in working tree at 2026-04-25 11:41:45; 132 score files generated under HEAD 16269a2 scorer + 138 under the modified scorer in the same run; for tokenize_arithmetic and top_k_by the modification touches a score-affecting code path (_extract_best_fix_line)

in_place_repairs_applied_during_R_A_audi: orphan repair: segments_above_threshold::T2::C4::gpt-5.4 index status running -> completed (response and score artifacts already on disk with status=ok score=1.0)
targeted re-score (under working-tree scorer; no score-affecting paths for these func_ids): chunk_on_change_T2_C2_claude-opus-4-6, chunk_on_change_T2_C2_gpt-5.4, find_balanced_spans_T2_C4_gpt-5.4

canonical_execution_state.evidence: git diff 9217a26 HEAD -- src/experiment/score_t2.py src/utils/llm_client.py returns 0 lines; all 300 score files have mtime > canonical commit timestamp 2026-04-26T14:42:37Z

attempts.planned_zero_explanation: The runner's `planned` counter reflects items currently in the `planned` lifecycle state. Once the runner has iterated to and started every item, no item remains in `planned`; the value 0 is the literal correct count, not an artifact inconsistency. The total executed is captured by `started=300` and `total_items=300` in the same index; cross-checked against audit.json and analysis_long.csv (300 rows).
attempts.execution_path_note: The launch was executed from /Users/takumi/Documents/GitHub/Lumen (the iCloud-Documents-managed working copy) rather than from the relocated /Users/takumi/Code/Lumen non-iCloud copy. Both copies were on HEAD 140ba95 with byte-identical canonical execution code. The iCloud sync risk did not materialize during the 36-minute run window; nevertheless, future long-running invocations should use the non-iCloud path as the safer environment per the Phase 4 preflight guidance.
